articles.title: Odisha Schools to Reopen for Classes 1–12 from June 18 After Heatwave Eases - News Room Odisha
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar: In a significant relief for students and parents, the Government of Odisha has announced that all schools across the state including both government and private institutions will reopen for students of Classes 1 to 12 from Thursday, June 18.
The decision has been taken in light of the latest forecast issued by the India Meteorological Department, which suggests a gradual easing of the severe heatwave conditions that had earlier led to the closure of schools in several districts.
School and Mass Education Minister Nityananda Gond confirmed the decision, stating that regular academic activities will resume across Bhubaneswar and other parts of the state from the reopening date.
However, the government has also issued a cautionary note, stating that the situation is being closely monitored. Officials added that if heatwave conditions worsen again, the schedule may be reviewed and the summer vacation could be extended further in the interest of student safety and well-being.

articles.title: Odisha Schools to Reopen June 18 as Heatwave Eases - Dailyhunt
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar: After weeks of sweltering heat kept classrooms shut, the Odisha government has announced the reopening of all schools from June 18. The decision covers both government and private institutions from Class 1 to 12.
The announcement was made on Monday by School and Mass Education Minister Nityananda Gond, following a forecast from the Met office predicting respite from the relentless heatwave.
Gond emphasized that the government remains cautious. If temperatures spike again, the vacation period could be extended to safeguard children's health.
Schools across the state had been closed early this summer due to soaring mercury levels. With conditions now expected to ease, classrooms are set to welcome back students this Thursday.
